title: lavandula stoechas var. pedunculata
intro: Lavandula stoechas var. pedunculata, also known as the Bunny Ear Lavender or Stoechad Lavender, is a subspecies of the Lavandula stoechas plant, characterized by its distinctive 'bunny ear' shaped flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its calming and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate. The medicinal properties of Lavandula stoechas var. pedunculata have been associated with a range of health benefits, including reduced stress and anxiety, improved sleep quality, and relief from menstrual cramps. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, essential oils, and topical creams, allowing for versatile applications in aromatherapy and natural health remedies.
